meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.definition: This major group comprises specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 0. The occupations classified under TEER 0, as those included in this group with zero (0) as the second digit of their code, are considered management occupations.
family.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.sub_major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.minor_group_title: Managers in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.definition: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of libraries, archives, museums, art galleries or departments within such institutions. They are employed in libraries, archives, museums and non-retail art galleries.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of libraries, archives, museums, art galleries or departments within such institutions.
employment_requirements: A graduate degree in archival science or history and several years of experience as an archivist or in historical research, including supervisory experience are required.
A graduate degree in library and information science andseveral years of experience as a librarian, including supervisory experience are required.
additional_information: There is mobility between library and archive managers and between museum and art gallery managers.
Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers may specialize in a specific type of collection.

core_competencies.statement: to maintain and enhance the institution’s collections, overseeing the acquisition, preservation and utilization of resources such as books, artifacts and artworks
to negotiate with donors on funding and scope of future activities, persuade stakeholders of the value of proposed initiatives and form partnerships with other institutions
to identify problems related to operations, collections or staff and determine the best course of action
to organize diverse types of materials, such as books, artifacts, or artworks, so that collections are accessible, well-maintained and searchable
to inspire and motivate their team, manage performance and drive the institution’s vision and mission forward

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Library, Conservation, and Heritage
core_competencies.statement: to understand and evaluate the activities of a library, museum, art gallery or archive system and to developp an interpretation of materials while meeting the needs of diverse audiences and stakeholders

units.title: Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.sub_major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.minor_group_title: Managers in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.definition: Managers in publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate operations within publishing firms, film, theatre and record production companies and broadcasting facilities. They are employed by radio and television stations, by newspaper, periodical and book publishing firms, and by film, theatre, record and video production companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate operations within publishing firms, film, theatre and record production companies and broadcasting facilities.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in communications, broadcasting, journalism or other arts discipline is usually required.
Several years of experience within the publishing, film production, broadcasting or theatre industry are usually required.
workplaces_employers: Film, theatre, record and video production companies
Newspaper, periodical and book publishing firms

example_titles.job_title_text: administrator of studio and broadcasting station

core_competencies.statement: to ensure equipment is maintained and available when required, optimizing the use of physical space and ensuring the success of publishing and media projects
to persuade others to support their projects and ideas or to negotiate terms with funders in order to reach favourable agreements
to recognize issues, such as delayed film production, analyze root causes of budget overruns, scheduling conflicts, creative differences or technical glitches, and develop effective solutions
to organize and prioritize large volumes of information, such as scripts, budgets, schedules and contracts, and adapt to changing priorities and emerging trends
to motivate their teams, resolve conflicts and make strategic decisions, whether they are working on a publishing project, a film set, or a live performance
to effectively plan and oversee operations, allocate resources, manage budgets and coordinate teams in publishing, broadcasting and performing arts

units.title: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.sub_major_group_title: Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.minor_group_title: Managers in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.definition: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of comprehensive recreational, sports and fitness programs and services, national or provincial sports governing agencies and professional athletic teams. They are employed by municipalities, community and private recreational and fitness organizations, sports governing agencies and professional athletic team organizations.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the operations of comprehensive recreational, sports and fitness programs and services, national or provincial sports governing agencies and professional athletic teams.
employment_requirements: A university degree in recreology, physical education, sports administration or a related field ora college diploma in recreation management or sports administration is usually required.
Executive directors of sports governing agencies may be required to have coaching certification in a particular sport.
Several years of experience in an occupation related to recreation and sports administration, consulting or programming are usually required.
Some directors of recreation may be required to have a Municipal Recreation Director's Certificate.
workplaces_employers: Community and private recreational and fitness organizations

example_titles.job_title_text: professional track and field team general manager
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) general director
professional track and field team manager
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) general secretary
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) programs director

core_competencies.statement: to oversee equipment, facilities or budgets while delivering high-quality programs and services while staying within budget constraints
to secure contracts with vendors, obtain sponsors' support for their events and convince stakeholders of the value of their programs
to develop innovative and engaging recreational and fitness programs in an ever-changing and competitive field
to articulate program or organizational goals, provide precise instructions to employees, motivate community participants and present concise ideas to stakeholders or the public
to understand how to create a cohesive work environment and motivate employees to provide high quality community and sports programs

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Recreation, Leisure and Fitness
core_competencies.statement: to understand current trends, participant needs, best practices in the field and how to design programs that are safe, enjoyable and beneficial for participants
